what_it_does: The HTML entry page for the Virtual Hub UI kit viewer. It sets up a single `#app` mount node, loads React 18.3.1 + ReactDOM + Babel-standalone from unpkg CDN (with SRI integrity hashes), links two stylesheets (`../../colors_and_type.css` and local `hub.css`), and then loads eight `text/babel` JSX component scripts in order (HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, CaptureComment, HubApp).
observations: Carries a `@dsCard` HTML comment with group/name/subtitle/viewport metadata describing it as 'Buyer-side panotour overlay · MENU · Info · Floorplan · Capture'.
Declared viewport in the dsCard is 1280x720.
Loads React, ReactDOM, and Babel from `unpkg.com` with explicit `integrity` SRI hashes and `crossorigin="anonymous"`.
Uses `@babel/standalone` to transpile JSX in the browser (no build step).
Eight component scripts loaded via `<script type="text/babel" src=...>`, with HubApp.jsx last (the composing root).
Links `../../colors_and_type.css` (shared design tokens) plus a local `hub.css`.
standouts: The `@dsCard` metadata comment, which lets the design-system gallery catalogue this page.
CDN-loaded React + browser Babel transpilation — a no-bundler prototype delivery.
Pinned dependency versions with SRI integrity hashes.
conventions: Follows the dsCard cataloguing convention used by the design corpus gallery.
Links the shared `colors_and_type.css` token sheet, aligning with the 'define once, reference everywhere' design-system rule.
Script load order is explicit and deliberate (components first, HubApp root last).
concerns: External CDN dependencies (unpkg) contradict self-contained delivery and would fail offline or under a strict CSP.
Browser-side Babel transpilation is a prototype/dev pattern, not production-grade.
notes: The viewer shell that assembles eight JSX overlay components into a buyer-side panotour 'virtual hub' demo, transpiled in-browser via CDN React+Babel.
questions: Where is the React root actually mounted onto `#app` — presumably inside HubApp.jsx, not shown here?
Is the unpkg/Babel-in-browser approach intended only for prototype viewing, with a separate production build elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the dsCard subtitle (panotour overlay with MENU/Info/Floorplan/Capture) lines up with the component set being loaded.
apparent_themes: Design-system gallery cataloguing via dsCard metadata
intention_signals: To provide a runnable, catalogue-able viewer page that wires together the virtual-hub overlay's eight UI components against the shared design tokens, without a build pipeline.
novelty: The combination of dsCard gallery metadata, SRI-pinned CDN React, and ordered browser-Babel JSX loading as a single self-describing kit entry page.
contribution: Contributes the composition/entry contract for the virtual-hub kit — the exact component set, their load order, the shared+local stylesheet wiring, and the gallery dsCard descriptor for this overlay.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML viewer page mounting a Virtual Hub panotour overlay UI kit by loading eight JSX components via CDN React and browser Babel, with shared design tokens and dsCard gallery metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To run and catalogue the buyer-side virtual-hub panotour overlay demo by composing its eight UI components against the shared design system without a build step.
